title: Flash Refugee Response Plan: Refugees in the Islamic Republic of Iran, March–May 2026
text: Iran hosts the largest refugee population in the sub-region, the vast majority from Afghanistan. The recent military escalation has triggered significant internal population movements. According to the Islamic Republic of Iran, 600,000 to one million households are estimated to have temporarily left their residences due to the conflict. Refugees, other Afghans, and host communities in Iran are struggling with concerns for their safety, job loss, psychological distress, and urgent shelter needs. Ongoing conflict and frequent aerial strikes make it harder to move around and reach humanitarian services.
